<!--
  Title must follow Conventional Commits, e.g.:
    feat(api): add pagination to list endpoints
    fix: handle empty payload on webhook
  See docs/08-pull-requests-and-reviews.md
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do, and why? One or two sentences. -->

## Type of change

- [ ] `feat` — new feature
- [ ] `fix` — bug fix
- [ ] `docs` — documentation only
- [ ] `refactor` — no behaviour change
- [ ] `ci` / `build` / `chore` — tooling, pipeline, maintenance
- [ ] Breaking change (add `!` to the title, e.g. `feat!: ...`)

## Related issues

<!-- Use closing keywords so issues auto-close on merge: -->
Closes #

## How was this tested?

<!-- Steps taken, environments used, evidence (logs/screenshots). -->

## Checklist

- [ ] PR targets the correct base branch (`dev` for features/fixes — see docs/02-branching-strategy.md)
- [ ] Title follows Conventional Commits
- [ ] Linked to an issue and/or milestone where applicable
- [ ] Docs updated if behaviour or interfaces changed
- [ ] No secrets, credentials, or personal data in the diff
